label: Self-supervised learning

node.label: Self-supervised learning
node.definition: Training targets are constructed from the data itself, such as masked regions, future tokens, or paired views.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Self-supervised learning is modeled as depending on Unsupervised learning, Tokens, vocabularies & symbolic units and feeding downstream nodes such as Contrastive learning, Predictive representation learning / JEPA, In-context & test-time learning.
examples: atlas downstream link to Contrastive learning
atlas downstream link to Predictive representation learning / JEPA
atlas downstream link to In-context & test-time learning
limitations: The chosen learning signal and interaction pattern may not match the deployment objective or data-generating process. Its direct dependencies include Unsupervised learning, Tokens, vocabularies & symbolic units.

dependencies.rationale: Self-supervised learning follows the learning setup expressed by Unsupervised learning.

In this atlas graph, Self-supervised learning has a `uses` dependency on Tokens, vocabularies & symbolic units because Tokens, vocabularies & symbolic units is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
In this atlas graph, Self-supervised learning has a `uses` dependency on Sequences, events & time series because Sequences, events & time series is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
In this atlas graph, Self-supervised learning has a `uses` dependency on Images, video & spatial grids because Images, video & spatial grids is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
Self-supervised learning needs sampled data batches to construct masking, pairing, or prediction targets from the observed data itself.
